description: Vietnamese (Northern / Hanoi) language pack for adult real-world learning. Meets the §10.1 MVP Seed Pack Target for text content in full: 300 vocabulary, 75 core phrases, 25 grammar points, 25 dialogues, 20+ slang/register items, 20 pronunciation/tone drills, 10 listening exercises, 10 writing prompts, 10 assessments, 5 roleplay scenarios. Speech grading is intentionally out of scope for this pack version (see editorialNotes).
coverage: A1-A2 everyday + adult real-world speech, built around what makes Vietnamese hard for English speakers: the six-tone Northern system (ngang, huyền, sắc, hỏi, ngã, nặng) taught as a first-class 'tone guide' with pitch contours and the classic six-way minimal set (ma / mà / má / mả / mã / mạ); the glottalized ngã and nặng tones with no English analog; the kinship-based pronoun/address system (anh/chị/em/ông/bà/con...) as a first-class social topic, since Vietnamese has no neutral 'I/you'; vowels with no English equivalent (ơ, ư, â); and the Northern d/gi/r = /z/ merger. Grammar covers the analytic A1-A2 core: no conjugation/plurals/articles/gender, subject-verb-object order, tense via đã/đang/sẽ and time words, classifiers (cái/con/chiếc...), question formation (không / à / question words / có...không), negation with không/chưa, comparatives with hơn/nhất, and the pronoun-driven register system.
editorialNotes: Northern (Hanoi) Vietnamese only — Southern (Saigon) and Central variation is out of scope for this pack version (a scope decision, flagged in NATIVE_REVIEW.md). Note the biggest North/South split is tonal: Southern speakers merge hỏi and ngã into one tone (a 5-tone system), so the six-tone guide here is specifically Northern. SPEECH GRADING IS DELIBERATELY DISABLED for Vietnamese: no reliable off-the-shelf pronunciation scorer grades lexical tone (meaning-bearing, and the glottalized ngã/nặng especially), so the pronunciation surface is a display-only 'tone guide' (listen-and-repeat, ungraded) rather than a scored exercise. The lemma orthography (chữ Quốc ngữ) already carries tone in its diacritics, so no separate romanization is needed; every diacritic is meaning-bearing and must be copied exactly. Identity-based slurs were deliberately EXCLUDED from the profanity set pending explicit owner review, same policy as pt-br. See NATIVE_REVIEW.md.
